summary: Closed, unmerged batch PR attempting to match several functions, mainly in the camera module, with many cross-module build/header fix-ups. The strongest evidence is for camera work: new C bodies or improved matches for Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002F274, Camera_8002F9E4, Camera_80030E44, plus Camera_8002B0E0 and Camera_8002FC7C match improvements. It also cleaned UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes across cm/mn/ty/ef/mp headers, added missing includes/prototypes, renamed ftCo ItemScope transition naming, and reverted or marked some unstable files NonMatching. The PR was not merged and the diff slice contains signs of batch/rebase risk, including unresolved conflict markers in tylist.c, so reuse should focus on the matching/header tactics rather than treating the whole branch as accepted.

key_files.role: Main matching work; added or improved camera routines including Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002F274, Camera_8002F9E4, Camera_80030E44, Camera_8002FC7C, Camera_8002FEEC, and Camera_8002B0E0.
Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS camera prototypes with typed signatures and added grlib-dependent caller support.
Added tylist scaffolding and bodies for un_803127D4 and fn_80314504, but the diff excerpt contains unresolved conflict markers, making this file unsafe as-is.
Added baselib forward include and fn_80314504 prototype.
Removed a large block of grInishie1 code from the diff; likely part of reverting unstable/nonmatching work, but exact intent is weakly evidenced.
Header cleanup: parameter naming and prototype clarification for effect library functions.
Small cleanup removing a dead/commented generator type-mask line.
Added static forward declarations and updated call site to the renamed ItemScope transition function.
Renamed fn_800D78B0 to ftCo_ItemScopeStart_GroundToAir with a brief behavior comment.
Added fn_80238540 body according to diff search; PR text says this was a 99.2% match and involved mndiagram config fixes.
Large header signature cleanup replacing unknown prototypes with more specific ones.
Added missing cobj.h include per PR text.
Added missing gobjproc.h include per PR text.

what_changed: Matched or improved several camera functions, with PR text claiming 100% matches for Camera_8002B0E0, Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002F274, and Camera_8002ED9C.

Added a C implementation for Camera_8002F9E4 at a claimed 85.4% match, setting camera mode, stage camera bounds, camera angle limits, pause eye/up vectors, min/max distance, and transform target interest/position.
Improved Camera_8002FC7C from a claimed 71.8% to 98.2% by introducing local temporaries and pointer aliases for cm_80452C68 fields, including x304 and transform access.
Added Camera_80030E44 at a claimed 90% match, using grLib_801C9CEC and camera arrays to register/update camera-related stage/event data.
Adjusted Camera_8002B0E0 from Player_GetPlayerId(0) & 0xFFFFu to & 0xFFu and removed an empty duplicate condition block.
Improved Camera_8002FEEC slightly according to PR text, with local variable ordering and PAD_STACK adjustment visible in the diff.
Added gr/grlib.h include to camera.c to support grLib_801C9CEC use.

Updated many camera.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures such as void Camera_800293E0(void), void Camera_8002C5B4(void*), void Camera_8002CB0C(CameraBounds*), s32 Camera_8002E234(void), and void Camera_800311EC(HSD_GObj*).
Cleaned effect library declarations by adding parameter names and removing a dead commented-out bitmask line in eflib.c.
Added missing static forward declarations in ftCo_Attack100.c for fn_800DA8E4, fn_800DAC78, and related build requirements.
Renamed fn_800D78B0 to ftCo_ItemScopeStart_GroundToAir and updated ftCo_ItemScopeStart_Coll to pass the named function to ft_800841B8.
Added or fixed prototypes/includes in gr, lb, mn, mp, ty, and vi headers/source files to resolve build and link issues.
The PR body lists multiple reversions or NonMatching markings for unstable files, including ftKirby, lbaudio_ax, lbrefract, mnname, tydisplay, tyfigupon, toy, and grcastle.
The tylist.c diff adds includes, globals, forward declarations, un_803127D4, and fn_80314504, but also visibly includes unresolved merge conflict markers.
smart_moves: Used typed header cleanup as a prerequisite for matching: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS exposed caller expectations and reduced accidental ABI/signature mismatches.
For camera matching, introduced local aliases and pointer temporaries to influence register allocation and load/store ordering without changing behavior.
Preserved exact low-level struct access patterns where needed, including byte-offset casts such as writing a s16 through cm_80452C68 + 0x35E and casting pad_342 storage as Vec3 or callback pointers.
Used small, evidence-based behavioral names when a function role was clear; ftCo_ItemScopeStart_GroundToAir is more searchable and useful than fn_800D78B0.
Added missing includes close to their users, for example gr/grlib.h in camera.c and baselib/cobj.h where CObj types/functions were needed.
Reverted or marked unstable files NonMatching to avoid blocking a build on unrelated incomplete work, although this PR still appears too broad and was not merged.
Kept match-progress percentages in commit descriptions, which makes it easier for future workers to triage whether a function is ready for review or still needs codegen work.
decomp_lessons: Do not batch too many unrelated systems into one matching PR; this slice spans cm, ef, ft, gr, lb, mn, mp, ty, and vi, and the PR closed without merge.
Before matching a function, align all visible prototypes with actual caller usage; stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations can hide wrong return types, argument types, and include dependencies.
Camera code often requires matching global struct field access order very closely. Local pointer aliases to cm_80452C68 subfields can be a legitimate matching tactic.
Bitfield and packed-storage accesses in Camera should be modeled carefully, especially cm_80452C68.x341_b*_b*, X35C_BITS, pad_342, and x35C union-like storage.

When a function stores different kinds of data in the same global buffer, as Camera_8002E818 appears to do with Vec3, player id, and callback pointer cases, preserve the original casting/storage pattern until the struct layout is better understood.

Stage/camera initialization functions may need many stage-boundary getters together: Stage_GetCamBoundsLeftOffset, RightOffset, TopOffset, BottomOffset, and camera angle getter calls appear as a coherent setup group.
Adding parameter names in headers is useful when names encode discovered semantics, but avoid cosmetic churn across many modules unless it directly supports matching or build correctness.
Generated or batch branches must be checked for conflict markers before review; tylist.c in this diff contains <<<<<<<, =======, and >>>>>>> markers.
A decomp.dev report saying 'No changes' is not the same as human approval; here there were no review comments and no merge.
If build stability requires reverting files to NonMatching, isolate those reversions in a separate cleanup PR where possible so matching evidence stays reviewable.
naming_conventions: Camera functions still use address-based names such as Camera_8002F9E4, Camera_80030E44, and Camera_8002E818 when semantics are not fully known.
Fighter common functions can receive semantic names once behavior is understood; fn_800D78B0 was renamed to ftCo_ItemScopeStart_GroundToAir.
The ftCo_ prefix is used for fighter common state/helper functions, and the new name encodes state/action plus transition direction.
Unknown menu/toy/video functions remain address-based with un_ or fn_ prefixes, such as un_803127D4, fn_80314504, fn_80238540, and fn_8031F600.
Header parameter names used arg_gobj, arg_jobj, arg_vec3, and effect where semantics were modestly understood but not enough for domain-specific names.
Camera global fields remain provisional offset-style names such as x304, x32C, x314, x35C, x341_b1_b2, x341_b3_b4, and pad_342 when struct meaning is not yet fully established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mask width matters for generated code and behavior: Camera_8002B0E0 changed Player_GetPlayerId(0) masking from 0xFFFFu to 0xFFu.
Empty source-level conditionals can be removed when they only existed as a failed matching artifact and do not correspond to needed codegen.
Local pointer aliases, such as x304_ptr, x32C_ptr, x314_ptr, pause_eye_ptr, and transform, were used to shape loads/stores and improve match percentage.
Explicit temporary variables like x304_check can force a byte load into a local before comparison, matching assembly better than directly comparing a struct field.
Reordering local declarations and adjusting PAD_STACK can slightly improve stack layout and match quality, as seen in Camera_8002FEEC.
A deliberate dummy/global reference comment 'Force load' was used in Camera_8002F9E4 to force a load of cm_803BCCA0.
Direct casts into global struct storage, such as *(Vec3*)&cm_80452C68.pad_342[2] and *(s16*)((u8*)&cm_80452C68 + 0x35E), were used to match packed or not-yet-modeled layout.
Switch statements with fall-through/default structure were retained in camera target-interest/target-position functions, likely to match original branch layout.
Initializing Vec3 fields in z, y, x order rather than x, y, z can be a codegen tactic and should not be normalized without checking asm.
Function-pointer storage in global data was modeled via pointer-to-function-pointer casts for callback cases in Camera_8002E818.

review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 stated 'No changes'. This is automated output only and does not provide human review guidance.
No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so none of these changes should be treated as accepted upstream without revalidation.
tylist.c contains unresolved merge conflict markers in the diff excerpt, an important quality issue for any future reuse.

follow_up_queries: Check whether any of Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002F274, Camera_8002F9E4, Camera_8002FC7C, or Camera_80030E44 were later merged in a smaller PR.
Search later PRs by ftCo_ItemScopeStart_GroundToAir to see whether the semantic rename was accepted.
Audit camera.h for the same UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS replacements and confirm which signatures survived on master.
Investigate tylist.c around un_803127D4 and fn_80314504 in later history because this PR's diff contains conflict markers.
Search for follow-up PRs from head_ref batch/20251231 or author itsgrimetime that split the deferred 195 commits.
Verify whether fn_80238540 in mnname.c reached a 100% match or was reverted as NonMatching in later work.
Check grInishie1_801FC110 and related removed grinishie1.c code to determine whether it was a bad match, a merge artifact, or later recovered.
Review later camera struct layout work for cm_80452C68.x35C, x341 bitfields, and pad_342 to replace raw casts with named union/struct fields.
